title: Add pointer influence to scene motion
summary: The pointer pipeline is 95% wired: pointer.ts tracks mouse/touch, mappings.ts converts speed to pointerDisturbance (0-1), and particleField.ts applies it. The gap is that disturbance drops to 0 instantly on idle — there's no decay. The fix is EMA smoothing in the render loop (matching the treble smoothing pattern) plus richer particle response to pointer position/direction.

existing_infrastructure.pointer_tracking: src/input/pointer.ts — fully implemented (US-006). Returns { x, y, dx, dy, speed, active }. Mouse + touch supported.
existing_infrastructure.mapping: src/visual/mappings.ts:166-170 — pointerToDisturbance() scales speed*10, clamps 0-1, returns 0 when inactive.
existing_infrastructure.render_loop_integration: src/visual/renderLoop.ts:106 — polls getPointerState() each frame, passes to mapSignalsToVisuals().
existing_infrastructure.particle_response: src/visual/systems/particleField.ts:65-67 — pushes particles perpendicular to velocity by pointerDisturbance * 0.002 * delta.
existing_infrastructure.evolution: src/visual/evolution.ts — passes pointerDisturbance through unchanged (not evolved).

gaps: No decay/smoothing — disturbance jumps to 0 the instant pointer stops moving
Particle push is uniform direction (sign of velocity) — no spatial awareness of pointer position

steps.title: Add EMA smoothing for pointer disturbance in render loop
steps.description: Add a `smoothDisturbance` state variable (like `smoothTreble` on line 81). After computing params, apply EMA: `smoothDisturbance = smoothDisturbance * 0.92 + params.pointerDisturbance * 0.08`. Override `params.pointerDisturbance` with the smoothed value. The 0.92 decay factor means ~50 frames (~0.8s at 60fps) to fade to near-zero, giving a perceptible but gentle decay.
changes: After params computation (after line 117/121), apply: `smoothDisturbance = smoothDisturbance * 0.92 + params.pointerDisturbance * 0.08; params.pointerDisturbance = smoothDisturbance;`
steps.rationale: Matches the existing treble smoothing pattern. Satisfies AC: 'Effect decays when pointer becomes idle' and 'Idle mode still looks intentional'.

steps.title: Enrich particle field pointer response with directional influence
steps.description: Replace the uniform perpendicular push (lines 65-67) with a position-aware radial push: particles near the pointer position get pushed away from it, creating a visible 'wake' effect. Particles far from the pointer are less affected. This makes the influence feel organic rather than uniform.
changes: Add pointerX and pointerY to FrameState (in types.ts) or pass through VisualParams
In draw(): compute distance from each particle to pointer position, apply inverse-distance-scaled push
Keep the disturbance magnitude scaling so decay still controls overall intensity
Fallback: if no pointer position available, use current uniform push
steps.rationale: Makes pointer influence spatially perceptible — viewer can feel their cursor affecting nearby particles without direct control. Satisfies AC: 'Pointer changes at least one motion parameter' with richer behavior.

steps.title: Thread pointer position through to particle field
steps.description: Add `pointerX` and `pointerY` (normalized 0-1) to FrameState so the geometry system can use pointer position for spatial effects. Populate from the polled pointer state in the render loop.
changes: Add `pointerX?: number; pointerY?: number;` to FrameState interface in types.ts
In renderLoop.ts, pass `pointerX: pointer.x, pointerY: pointer.y` in the frame state object (line 136-143)
steps.rationale: FrameState already carries params/time/delta — adding pointer position keeps the data flow clean without polluting VisualParams with non-visual data.

steps.title: Write tests for decay behavior
steps.description: Add tests verifying: (a) disturbance is non-zero on the frame after pointer stops (decay, not instant drop), (b) disturbance approaches zero after many idle frames, (c) disturbance ramps up smoothly when pointer starts moving. Pattern after existing treble smoothing tests.
test_ids: T-013-01: pointerDisturbance decays gradually after pointer becomes idle
T-013-02: pointerDisturbance approaches zero after sustained idle period
T-013-03: pointerDisturbance ramps up smoothly on pointer movement start

steps.title: Write tests for spatial pointer influence on particles
steps.description: Add tests verifying: (a) particles near pointer position are displaced more than distant ones, (b) displacement direction is away from pointer, (c) zero disturbance produces no extra displacement, (d) touch and mouse produce equivalent effects (already guaranteed by pointer.ts but worth an integration assertion).
test_ids: T-013-04: particles near pointer position displace more than distant ones
T-013-05: zero pointerDisturbance produces no extra particle displacement
T-013-06: idle scene without pointer input still animates (intentional motion)

steps.title: Verify definition of done
steps.description: Run `npm run build`, load in browser, verify: no console errors, no forbidden storage APIs, mute toggle and info overlay work, scene renders with and without autoplay, pointer influence decays visibly, idle scene still looks intentional.
checks: Scene works with and without autoplay
Mute toggle and info overlay functional

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Pointer changes at least one motion para: Already true (pointerDisturbance). Step 2 enriches it with spatial influence.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Effect decays when pointer becomes idle: Step 1 — EMA smoothing with 0.92 decay factor.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Touch and mouse both influence the scene: Already handled by pointer.ts (US-006). No changes needed.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Idle mode still looks intentional: Base particle animation (velocity + cadence + bass + treble) continues regardless. Decay ensures smooth transition to idle. Step 2's spatial effect fades gracefully.

risks.risk: EMA decay rate feels wrong (too fast or too slow)
risks.mitigation: 0.92 is a starting point (~0.8s half-life). Tunable by adjusting the coefficient. Test visually.

risks.risk: Spatial pointer influence is too expensive per-frame for high particle counts
risks.mitigation: Distance calculation is just subtraction + multiply — no sqrt needed if we use squared distance. MAX_PARTICLES is 600, well within budget.
